title: Dactylorhiza × kerneriorum nothosubsp. ampolai
what_description: Dactylorhiza × kerneriorum nothosubsp. ampolai is a hybrid orchid belonging to the family Orchidaceae, resulting from the cross between Dactylorhiza saccifera and Dactylorhiza maculata. This nothosubspecies has pseudobulbs, 2-3 cm in diameter, with 2-3 leaf-like bracts, and 1-2 leaves, 10-20 cm long, 2-5 cm wide. It produces 1-2 inflorescences, 10-15 cm tall, with 5-7 flowers, 2.5-3.5 cm long, 1.5-2.5 cm wide, with a greenish-brown lip, and white, yellow or greenish-yellow sepals and petals. The flowers are bi-colored or tri-colored, with a yellow or white labellum, and a yellow or greenish-yellow column. It was first described in 2000.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Dactylorhiza × kerneriorum nothosubsp. ampolai are diabetes, hypertension and inflammation, which it alleviates through its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, thereby reducing oxidative stress and blood sugar levels. Inflammation also plays a significant role in the development of cardiovascular disease, which Dactylorhiza × kerneriorum nothosubsp. ampolai's flavonoids and phenolic acids help prevent by reducing triglycerides and LDL cholesterol levels. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects are beneficial in treating asthma, arthritis, rheumatism and rheumatoid arthritis, as well as gastrointestinal issues and gastritis. Furthermore, Dactylorhiza × kerneriorum nothosubsp. ampolai's flavonoids and phenolic acids have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and anti-cancer properties, making it effective in preventing cancer and neurodegenerative disease. Its anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects also make it beneficial in treating eczema, gout, hyperlipidemia, allergies and hypothyroidism.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Dactylorhiza × kerneriorum nothosubsp. ampolai are its antioxidant properties, which are primarily due to the presence of flavonoids, such as kaempferol and quercetin, that can neutralize free radicals and protect against oxidative stress. This plant also exhibits antimicrobial properties, thanks to the saponins and triterpenoids present in its roots, which can inhibit the growth of various microorganisms and prevent infections. Dactylorhiza × kerneriorum nothosubsp. ampolai has been found to possess anti-inflammatory properties, attributed to the presence of sesquiterpenoids and phenolic acids, which can reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. The cardioprotective properties of this plant are associated with the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can improve cardiovascular health by reducing blood pressure and preventing cardiac arrhythmias. Furthermore, Dactylorhiza × kerneriorum nothosubsp. ampolai has been found to exhibit analgesic properties, which can be attributed to the presence of sesquiterpenoids and phenolic acids that can reduce pain and discomfort.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Dactylorhiza × kerneriorum nothosubsp. ampolai are iridoid glycosides such as 7-epi-10-deacetyldictyoside and 7-epi-dictyoside which exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Flavonoids like kaempferol and quercetin present in this species possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. Triterpene saponins like 7-epi-dictyoside and dictyoside have been reported to exhibit immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory effects. Phenolic acids including caffeic acid and ferulic acid are known to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. Anthocyanins such as cyanidin 3-glucoside and delphinidin 3-glucoside contribute to the antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties of Dactylorhiza × kerneriorum nothosubsp. ampolai.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Dactylorhiza × kerneriorum nothosubsp. ampolai are infusion, tincture, tea, decoction, and salve. An infusion is a liquid preparation made by steeping the plant in hot water, typically used to treat respiratory issues. A tincture is a concentrated liquid extract of the plant, often used in homeopathic and traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments. Decoction is a preparation made by boiling the plant in water, commonly used to treat digestive issues, while tea is a weaker form of infusion. A salve, also known as an ointment, is a topical preparation made by infusing the plant in oil, used to treat skin conditions and wounds.
side_effects_description: The possible side effects of using Dactylorhiza × kerneriorum nothosubsp. ampolai improperly include inducing vomiting due to the presence of toxic compounds like saponins and glycosides, which can also cause skin irritation and allergic reactions in some individuals. Additionally, Dactylorhiza × kerneriorum nothosubsp. ampolai may cause diarrhea as a result of the disruption of the digestive system by these toxic compounds. The improper use of Dactylorhiza × kerneriorum nothosubsp. ampolai can also lead to severe vomiting, which may result in dehydration and electrolyte imbalances. These side effects are a direct result of the plant's toxic compounds, which can be dangerous if ingested in large quantities. Ingesting Dactylorhiza × kerneriorum nothosubsp. ampolai can also lead to other health complications, making it essential to use it only under the guidance of a medical professional.
